alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent segmentation methods based on convolutional neural networks that use a differentiable surrogate of the Dice score can lead to improved Dice scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent segmentation methods based on convolutional neural networks that use a differentiable surrogate of the Dice score can lead to improved Dice scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim text are effectively identical in content and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a differentiable surrogate of the Dice score can introduce a volumetric bias for tasks with high inherent uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a differentiable surrogate of the Dice score can introduce a volumetric bias for tasks with high inherent uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and certainty are the same between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of volumetric bias may limit the clinical applicability of these segmentation methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of volumetric bias may limit the clinical applicability of segmentation methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is retained verbatim (aside from minor wording) and keeps the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper focuses on neural-based estimators for the conditional mutual information term in communication systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper focuses on neural-based estimators for the conditional mutual information term in communication systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results provide a mathematically rigorous lower bound for the KL-divergence in the context of neural network-based encoders and decoders. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a mathematically rigorous lower bound for the conditional mutual information, contrasting with some previous works. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the target quantity from 'KL-divergence' to 'conditional mutual information', a substantive change in the technical claim though the overall type of result is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Additional challenges regarding the unconditional mutual information arise due to the presence of a conditional density function. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Additional challenges regarding the unconditional mutual information arise due to the presence of a conditional density function. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation statement is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed segmentor discriminator performs a patch-wise real/fake classification and is supervised by input masks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed segmentor discriminator performs a patch-wise real/fake classification supervised by input masks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of the proposed discriminator is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach achieves state-of-the-art performances on the publicly available DTD dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new approach achieves state-of-the-art performances on the DTD dataset and Electo-Magnetic borehole images dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by adding an additional dataset where SOTA performance is achieved, broadening the scope of the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method better deals with local consistency than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method better deals with local consistency in texture inpainting compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the domain ('texture inpainting') in which local consistency improvements hold, narrowing and clarifying the original, broader claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Resnet-50 model achieves the highest accuracy of 95% and zero false negative rates in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Resnet-50 model achieves the highest accuracy of 95% and the least false-negative rate of 0 in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance numbers and conclusion about ResNet-50 are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VGG-16 model achieves an accuracy of 90% in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VGG-16 model achieves an accuracy of 90% and the Inception-V3 model achieves an accuracy of 55% in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The VGG-16 accuracy is repeated verbatim in the publication's combined result claim, so the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Inception-V3 model achieves an accuracy of 55% in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VGG-16 model achieves an accuracy of 90% and the Inception-V3 model achieves an accuracy of 55% in classifying brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The Inception-V3 accuracy is included in the publication's consolidated result statement with identical values, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent advancements in deep learning enable the detection of cancerous tissue from brain MRI scans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in deep learning have made it possible to detect the growth of cancerous tissue from brain MRI scans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the certainty and slightly rephrases the conclusion (emphasizing 'made it possible'), so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network has been developed to solve the Grad-Shafranov equation and reconstruct magnetic equilibria in real time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural network has been developed to solve the Grad–Shafranov equation and reconstruct magnetic equilibria in real time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network fully reconstructs the poloidal flux function and the toroidal current density function with off-line EFIT quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed neural networks can fully reconstruct both the poloidal flux function and the toroidal current density function with off-line EFIT quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about full reconstruction with off-line EFIT quality is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An imputation scheme is utilized to enhance the robustness of the networks against missing input data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An imputation scheme is utilized to enhance the robustness of the networks against missing input data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The imputation-scheme conclusion is preserved in the published paper with the same certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed objective functions and network architectures enable training a source separation system with weak labels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed objective functions and network architectures enable training a source separation system with weak labels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with the same emphasis, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The separator estimates a time-frequency mask for each type of sound event using a sound event classifier to assess performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The separator estimates a time-frequency mask for each type of sound event using a sound event classifier to assess performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The objective function requires the classifier to assign high probability to the class corresponding to the separated source and low probability to all other classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The objective function requires the separator to estimate a source that the classifier will assign high probability to, while ensuring the separated sources sum up to the mixture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core requirement about classifier-assigned probability but adds an explicit mixing/summation constraint, clarifying the loss formulation without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves somewhat lower but still significant SI-SDR improvement compared to training a network using isolated sources. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compared to training a network using isolated sources, the model achieves somewhat lower but still significant SI-SDR improvement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental result with equivalent wording and emphasis, so meaning and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing models could not precisely capture the causes of specific driving actions. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing models could not precisely capture the causes of specific driving actions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation/result claim with equivalent meaning; only the label (type) differs but not the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Temporal Reasoning Block (TRB) was proposed and introduced to improve model performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Temporal Reasoning Block (TRB) was proposed and introduced to improve model performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is identically reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With the TRB models, we achieved an accuracy of 86.3%, outperforming state-of-the-art 3D CNNs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With the TRB models, we achieved an accuracy of 86.3%, outperforming state-of-the-art 3D CNNs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention saliency demonstrated that TRB helped models focus on the causes more precisely. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention saliency demonstrated that TRB helped models focus on the causes more precisely. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the attention-saliency result with the same interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed TRB models were able to provide accurate driving behavior prediction by learning the causal reasoning of the behaviors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed TRB models were able to provide accurate driving behavior prediction by learning the causal reasoning of the behaviors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed student-teacher framework effectively addresses unsupervised anomaly detection and pixel-precise anomaly segmentation in high-resolution images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed student-teacher framework effectively addresses unsupervised anomaly detection and pixel-precise anomaly segmentation in high-resolution images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution claim directly, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anomalies are detected when the outputs of the student networks differ from that of the teacher network, indicating a failure to generalize outside the manifold of anomaly-free training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anomalies are detected when the outputs of the student networks differ from that of the teacher network, indicating a failure to generalize outside the manifold of anomaly-free training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is presented the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intrinsic uncertainty in the student networks serves as an additional scoring function that indicates anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intrinsic uncertainty in the student networks serves as an additional scoring function that indicates anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this additional scoring mechanism claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method improves upon state-of-the-art methods for unsupervised anomaly detection on various real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method improves upon state-of-the-art methods for unsupervised anomaly detection on various real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority is claimed identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensory inputs drive a series of continuous phase transitions related to spontaneous intrinsic-symmetry breaking in unsupervised learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensory inputs drive a series of continuous phase transitions related to spontaneous intrinsic-symmetry breaking in unsupervised learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same result claim about phase transitions and symmetry breaking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prior knowledge reduces more strongly the minimal data size triggering the reverse symmetry breaking transition compared to a prior-free scenario. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prior knowledge reduces more strongly the minimal data size triggering the reverse-symmetry breaking transition compared to the prior-free scenario. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this quantitative effect of prior knowledge with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prior knowledge merges, rather than separates, permutation symmetry breaking phases in unsupervised learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prior knowledge merges, rather than separates, permutation-symmetry breaking phases in unsupervised learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding about how prior knowledge affects permutation-symmetry breaking phases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prior can be learned from data samples, corresponding to a two-parameter Nishimori constraint in physics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prior can be learned from data samples, corresponding to a two-parameter Nishimori constraint in physics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the interpretive claim linking a learnable prior to a Nishimori constraint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work reveals mechanisms about the influence of the prior on unsupervised learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study reveals mechanisms about the influence of the prior on unsupervised learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level contribution/conclusion about mechanisms, with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The boosted pipeline could turn out up to 5 times faster than the traditional pipeline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boosted pipeline could turn out up to 5 times faster than the traditional pipeline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The speedup claim is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recognition rate improves in most cases when using the boosted pipeline. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recognition rate improves in most cases when using the boosted pipeline. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The boosted pipeline exhibits only a slight decrease in recognition accuracy in some cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boosted pipeline exhibits only a slight decrease in recognition accuracy in some cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The boosted pipeline can speed-up processing time substantially with limited impacts or even benefits in recognition accuracy. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boosted pipeline can speed-up processing time substantially with limited impacts or even benefits in recognition accuracy. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text:
ViSTRA2 demonstrates consistent and significant compression gains against HM and VVC based on Bj{
o}negaard Delta measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViSTRA2 demonstrates consistent and significant compression gains against HM and VVC based on Bjønegaard Delta measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ViSTRA2 achieves average BD-rate savings of 12.6% (PSNR) and 19.5% (VMAF) over HM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViSTRA2 achieves average BD-rate savings of 12.6% (PSNR) and 19.5% (VMAF) over HM. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ViSTRA2 achieves average BD-rate savings of 5.5% (PSNR) and 8.6% (VMAF) over VTM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViSTRA2 achieves average BD-rate savings of 5.5% (PSNR) and 8.6% (VMAF) over VTM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fusion of noisy and incomplete fields from wind tunnel measurements with deterministic but biased fields from numerical simulations can estimate the true field that best matches measured quantities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fusion of noisy and incomplete fields from wind-tunnel measurements with deterministic but biased fields from numerical simulations can estimate the true field that best matches measured quantities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statement is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Bayesian framework is employed to infer the true fields conditioned on measured quantities of interest, performing a statistical correction to the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Bayesian framework is employed to infer the true fields conditioned on measured quantities of interest, providing a statistical correction to the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same methodological claim; wording changed minimally without altering meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian method is more robust when data is scarce and can account for uncertainties in the data compared to the Proper Orthogonal Decomposition method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian method is more robust when data are scarce and can account for uncertainties in the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Core claim retained but the publication raises the stated certainty (medium -> high) and omits the explicit comparison phrase; overall meaning preserved and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When adequate data is available, both the Proper Orthogonal Decomposition based and Bayesian approaches lead to similar results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Given adequate data, the proper-orthogonal-decomposition-based and Bayesian approaches lead to surprisingly similar results. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion but adds emphasis ('surprisingly') and presents it as a reported result; content is effectively the same with slight clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed EFU caching strategy achieves 20% higher hit ratio than the LRU strategy during SVM training with the Gaussian kernel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed EFU caching strategy achieves 20% higher hit ratio than the LRU strategy during SVM training with the Gaussian kernel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical experimental result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HCST caching strategy adaptively achieves high hit ratios with little runtime overhead across various multi-output learning problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid caching strategy for SVM training (HCST) adaptively achieves high hit ratios with little runtime overhead across various problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HCST achieves an average of 20% reduction in training time compared to other existing caching strategies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HCST results in an average reduction of 20% in training time compared to other existing caching strategies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric result and comparison are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Overfitting in machine learning models decreases the reliability of learning systems in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this specific ITS-focused claim about decreased reliability; related discussions exist but this exact conclusion is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models in ITS applications are more prone to overfitting due to their use of a large number of hyper-parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep networks have more hyper-parameters than shallow networks, which increases the probability of overfitting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same causal claim about hyper-parameters increasing overfitting risk but presents it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regularized learning models can be employed to address the issue of overfitting in different categories of ITS studies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review discusses the relationship between overfitting control, regularization, network compression, and network simplification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the point as a broader discussion of regularization and related methods rather than the prescriptive statement in the preprint, and the expressed certainty is lower.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A case study on driving safety demonstrates the effectiveness of a regularized version of the convolutional neural network (CNN). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No explicit case-study result demonstrating a regularized CNN's effectiveness appears in the publication; related themes are discussed but the specific claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The joint optimization of the sampling patterns and the reconstruction module significantly improves the performance of most deep learning reconstruction algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The joint optimization of the sampling patterns and the reconstruction module significantly improves the performance of most deep learning reconstruction algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A continuous strategy to jointly optimize the sampling pattern and network parameters is introduced. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A continuous strategy to optimize the sampling pattern and the network parameters jointly is introduced. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same methodological contribution stated in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image quality of MRI approaches relying on compressed sensing or deep learning algorithms is heavily dependent on the sampling pattern. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The image quality of MRI approaches is heavily dependent on the sampling pattern. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core conclusion about dependence on sampling pattern is unchanged (publication wording slightly more general).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PARAFAC2 can successfully reveal the underlying networks and their dynamics in time-evolving data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PARAFAC2 can successfully reveal the underlying networks and their dynamics in time-evolving data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are textually identical and retain the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates promising performance in tracing the evolution of task-related functional connectivity in the brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model shows promising performance in tracing the evolution of task-related functional connectivity in the brain. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication reduces stated certainty from high to medium, so the claim is unchanged semantically but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional approaches in neuroimaging data analysis often assume static spatial networks, which may not accurately reflect the dynamic behavior of complex systems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional approaches in neuroimaging data analysis often assume static spatial networks, which may not accurately reflect the dynamic behavior of complex systems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is identical between preprint and publication with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods have profoundly impacted molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods have profoundly affected molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained verbatim (minor wording change 'impacted' -> 'affected') with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent machine learning methods include deep neural networks for predicting quantum-mechanical energies and forces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent ML methods include deep neural networks for predicting quantum-mechanical energies and forces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is present in the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are open methodological problems in the application of machine learning to molecular simulations. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are open methodological problems that need to be addressed in the application of ML to molecular simulations. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identifies and describes open challenges at the interface between machine learning and molecular simulation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A list of open challenges for the interface between ML and molecular simulation has been identified. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Coarse-grained molecular dynamics and generative network approaches are significant applications of ML in molecular simulation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim about specific application areas (coarse-grained MD and generative networks) does not appear in the preprint and is therefore an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep autoencoder architecture can find a coordinate transformation that turns a nonlinear PDE into a linear PDE. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a deep autoencoder architecture that can find a coordinate transformation which turns a non-linear partial differential equation into a linear PDE. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates the ability to provide a robust architecture for discovering interpretable, linearizing transforms for nonlinear PDEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method provides a robust architecture for discovering linearising transforms for non-linear PDEs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting dynamics from the architecture are represented by a Koopman operator matrix. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting dynamics are given by a Koopman operator matrix K. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements explicitly state that the dynamics are represented by a Koopman operator matrix with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple time step prediction can be performed by repeated multiplication by the matrix in the intrinsic coordinates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention multi-step prediction via repeated multiplication; this specific operational claim is not present in the publication's claim list and is treated as removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate our method on a number of examples, including the heat equation and Burgers’ equation, as well as the Kuramoto–Sivashinsky equation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration on specific example PDEs does not appear among the preprint claims provided and therefore is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-agent inverse optimization method can learn network parameters using samples from one OD pair, resulting in a 0.23 correlation between forecasted and observed travel times. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The forecasted travel times using the MAIO method have a 0.23 correlation with the observed travel times when using one OD pair. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numeric result (0.23 correlation) for the one-OD-pair case directly matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the monitoring to two OD pairs improves the correlation between forecasted and observed travel times to 0.56. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing the monitoring to two OD pairs improves the correlation of forecasted travel times to 0.56. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The improvement to 0.56 with two OD pairs is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study validates the multi-agent inverse optimization method using taxi GPS probe data from Wuhan, China. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims enumerate method capability and correlation results but do not explicitly state the dataset/location (Wuhan taxi GPS), so this specific validation detail is not present in the publication claim list and is treated as removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The multiagent inverse optimization (MAIO) method can learn network parameters using samples from only one origin-destination (OD) pair. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the one-OD-pair capability as an isolated claim; while the preprint combined capability and numeric result, the publication lists the capability separately and it has no distinct matching preprint claim index in the provided set, so it is included as an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We could find feasible solutions for all instances and several optimal solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feasible solutions were found for all instances tested in the study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feasibility result is stated equivalently in the publication; the preprint also mentioned optimal solutions but the feasibility aspect maps directly to this publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using Very Large Neighborhood Search (VLNS) we can improve upon the results of the other approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Very Large Neighborhood Search (VLNS) approach improves upon the results of other approaches for the scheduling problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same improvement claim for VLNS with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different constraint programming models and search strategies were presented for the scheduling problem. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different constraint programming models and search strategies were developed for the scheduling problem. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing models and strategies is preserved between preprint and publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Very deep transformers have outperformed conventional bi-directional long short-term memory networks in speech recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Very deep transformers outperform conventional bi-directional long short-term memory networks for automatic speech recognition by a significant margin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but adds that the improvement is by a "significant margin," which clarifies and strengthens the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed non-autoregressive network framework can support different decoding strategies, including a new strategy that predicts from easiest to most difficult tokens. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Audio-Conditional Masked Language Model and Audio-Factorized Masked Language Model are effective non-autoregressive transformer structures for automatic speech recognition. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The paper shifts from a generic framework and a new decoding strategy to naming two specific non-autoregressive model variants and asserting their effectiveness, narrowing the claim to concrete architectures without changing stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperformed the Kaldi ASR system and matches the performance of the state-of-the-art autoregressive transformer with a 7x speedup on the Aishell benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperformed the Kaldi ASR system and matched the performance of the state-of-the-art autoregressive transformer with a $7\times$ speedup on the AISHELL benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical performance claim (including the 7x speedup) with essentially identical wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The WECC composite load model (WECC CLM) effectively captures dynamic load responses over traditional load models in various stability studies and contingency analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified load model demonstrates strong robustness in representing the reference load model under a wide range of contingencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes robustness under contingencies, which clarifies and concretizes the preprint's broader claim about capturing dynamic responses in stability and contingency studies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A double deep Q-learning network (DDQN)-based, two-stage load modeling framework for the WECC CLM is proposed to improve the identification of load parameters. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed double deep Q-learning network (DDQN)-based, two-stage load modeling framework effectively identifies and calibrates parameters of the WECC Composite Load Model (CLM). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same proposed DDQN two-stage framework but with stronger (high) certainty about its effectiveness in identification and calibration, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-stage method does not require explicit model details for the WECC CLM. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates or supports the assertion that the method does not require explicit model details; the claim appears to be omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The set of selected load parameters from the proposed framework is expected to best approximate the true transient responses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified load model accurately simulates the dynamics of the reference load model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication turns the preprint's expectation about parameter selection into an asserted accurate simulation of the reference model, clarifying and increasing confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated Learning enables training of a general model through edge devices without sending raw data to the cloud. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated Learning enables training of a general model through edge devices without sending raw data to the cloud. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about federated learning with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A CNN, a LSTM and a MLP were successfully trained on the MNIST data-set using Raspberry Pi4s as edge devices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A CNN, a LSTM, and a MLP were successfully trained on the MNIST data-set using Raspberry Pi4s as edge devices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With the CNN and the non-IID samples, a test-accuracy of up to 85% could be achieved within a training time of 2 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With the CNN and the non-IID samples, a test-accuracy of up to 85% could be achieved within a training time of 2 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical measurement unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning is demonstrated experimentally on IID and non-IID samples in a parametric study to benchmark the model convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated learning was demonstrated experimentally on IID and non-IID samples in a parametric study to benchmark model convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental demonstration and study are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discusses privacy risks and labeling requirements for the application of emotion detection from sound. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study discusses privacy risks and labeling requirements for the application of emotion detection from sound. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The discussion of privacy and labeling requirements is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A small but expressive programming language for reverse-mode differentiation is defined. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small but expressive programming language for automatic differentiation is defined. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the claim from reverse-mode specific to automatic differentiation more generally, a revision that increases scope while keeping confidence high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Operational and denotational semantics for the defined programming language are provided. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The operational and denotational semantics of the programming language coincide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication goes beyond merely providing both semantics to asserting their coincidence; this clarifies and strengthens the relationship between the two semantics described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The operational semantics employs popular implementation techniques. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The operational semantics of the programming language employs popular implementation techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The operational-semantics claim is restated in the publication with essentially the same meaning but with stronger expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The denotational semantics employs notions of differentiation familiar from real analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The denotational semantics of the programming language employs notions of differentiation familiar from real analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the denotational-semantics claim but with higher expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The operational and denotational semantics for the programming language coincide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The operational and denotational semantics of the programming language coincide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The key conclusion that the two semantics coincide is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed semi-heterogeneous three-way joint embedding network (Semi3-Net) integrates three branches to learn more discriminative cross-domain feature representations for the SBIR task. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed semi-heterogeneous three-way joint embedding network (Semi3-Net) integrates three branches to learn more discriminative cross-domain feature representations for the SBIR task. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this architectural contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A joint semantic embedding is introduced to embed the features from different domains into a common high-level semantic space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A joint semantic embedding is introduced to embed the features from different domains into a common high-level semantic space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The joint semantic embedding contribution is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods on two widely used category-level datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods on two widely used category-level datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical superiority on the two datasets is reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hybrid-loss mechanism is designed to align the three branches, encouraging the network to learn invariant cross-domain representations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hybrid-loss mechanism is designed to align the three branches, encouraging the network to learn invariant cross-domain representations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The design and role of the hybrid-loss mechanism are carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian recurrent neural network model overcomes the shortcomings of current methods for long-term trajectory prediction by ensuring the physical feasibility of predicted distributions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian recurrent neural network model overcomes the shortcomings of current methods for long-term trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim but omits the explicit phrase about 'ensuring the physical feasibility' in Q0 (that detail appears separately in Q2), so the main claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian neural network can ensemble complicated output distributions, enabling a rich family of trajectory distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian neural network can ensemble complicated output distribution, enabling a rich family of trajectory distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are essentially identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The embedded physical model within the proposed method ensures the feasibility of the trajectory distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The embedded physical model ensures the feasibility of the predicted trajectory distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about the embedded physical model ensuring feasible predicted distributions with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gradient-based training method adopted in the model allows for direct optimization, leading to better performance in long prediction horizons. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradient-based training method allows for direct optimization, improving performance in long prediction horizons. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same point about gradient-based training improving long-horizon performance with equivalent confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A particle-filter-based parameter adaptation algorithm is designed to adapt the policy Bayesian neural network to the predicted target online. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The particle-filter-based parameter adaptation algorithm effectively adapts the policy Bayesian neural network to the predicted target online. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same particle-filter adaptation; the publication slightly shifts wording to emphasize effective adaptation (framing as a result), so this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed TAMP algorithm optimizes over Cartesian frames defined relative to target objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed TAMP algorithm optimizes over Cartesian frames defined relative to target objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and framing in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting plan from the TAMP algorithm remains valid even if the objects are moving. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting plan from the TAMP algorithm remains valid even if the objects are moving. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TAMP framework can be executed by reactive controllers that adapt to changes in real time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TAMP framework can be executed by reactive controllers that adapt to changes in real time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct repetition of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TAMP framework demonstrates the ability to adapt to changing environments, inaccurate perception, and imprecise control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TAMP framework demonstrates the ability to adapt to changing environments, inaccurate perception, and imprecise control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Locally Aligned Convolutional Neural Network (LACNN) model predicts possible landslide occurrences by following the ground surface at multiple scales. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Locally Aligned Convolutional Neural Network (LACNN) model predicts possible landslide occurrences by following the ground surface at multiple scales. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LACNN achieves a 2-7% improvement in accuracy compared to several baseline models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LACNN achieves a 2-7% improvement in accuracy compared to several baseline models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance improvement figures and claim are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LACNN provides a 2-15% boost in log likelihood compared to other proposed baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LACNN provides a 2-15% boost in log likelihood compared to other proposed baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Log-likelihood improvements are reported the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution can predict training throughput from profiling traces collected from a single-node configuration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution can predict training throughput from profiling traces collected from a single-node configuration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this primary prediction capability claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach models the interaction of multiple nodes and the scheduling of concurrent transmissions between the parameter server and each node. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach models the interaction of multiple nodes and the scheduling of concurrent transmissions between the parameter server and each node. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution is described equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method predicts overlaps between computation and communication and generates synthetic execution traces for configurations with multiple nodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method predicts overlaps between computation and communication and generates synthetic execution traces for configurations with multiple nodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same technical result appears in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation of the approach was conducted on TensorFlow training jobs for popular image classification neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation of the approach was conducted on TensorFlow training jobs for popular image classification neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Validation details are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates the effects of data transmission policies used in TensorFlow and the accuracy of the approach when combined with optimizations of the transmission schedule. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This specific investigative/interpretive claim does not appear in the publication; the paper omits discussion about transmission-policy effects and combined-optimization accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach effectively detects glare in images taken by users via mobile devices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach effectively detects glare in images taken by users via mobile devices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Core detection effectiveness claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes luminance features and black-white strokes histograms for glare detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes luminance features and black-white strokes histograms for glare detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The feature/design claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network used in the method consists of several feature extraction blocks and a detection block. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the specific architectural description of the CNN (feature-extraction and detection blocks) present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution achieves high recall and f-score in glare detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network used in the method achieves high recall and f-score in glare detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved but the publication attributes it specifically to the CNN, a slight rephrasing that clarifies the agent achieving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper visualizes concerned areas of human faces in emotion recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper visualizes concerned areas of human faces in emotion recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution statement verbatim, with no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study analyzes the contribution of different face areas to different emotions in real-world conditions through experimental analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contributions of different face areas to different emotions can be analyzed in real-world conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about analyzing face-area contributions under real-world conditions with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings can be combined with findings in psychology to promote the understanding of emotional expressions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings of this research can be combined with findings in psychology to enhance the understanding of emotional expressions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion (combining with psychology to improve understanding), using synonymous wording without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCA-GLUE is a fast method for calculating an approximate time delay estimation (TDE) between two radio frequency frames of ultrasound.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PCA-GLUE is a fast method for calculating an approximate time delay estimation (TDE) between two radio frequency frames of ultrasound.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution/result without altering scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCA-GLUE is more than ten times faster than Dynamic Programming (DP) in the estimation of the initial displacement field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PCA-GLUE is more than ten times faster than Dynamic Programming (DP) in the estimation of the initial displacement field.
alignments.rationale: The speed-up claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCA-GLUE yields similar results to those obtained using Dynamic Programming (DP) for TDE estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PCA-GLUE yields similar results to those obtained using Dynamic Programming (DP) for TDE estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the equivalence claim between PCA-GLUE and DP with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCA-GLUE utilizes Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to find general modes of deformation in quasi-static elastography.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this methodological statement about PCA finding general deformation modes, so it appears omitted in the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method for tracking RF frames and selecting the best possible pair improves the quality of strain images in quasi-static elastography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for tracking RF frames and selecting the best possible pair improves the quality of strain images in quasi-static elastography.
alignments.rationale: The main result about improved strain-image quality is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-layer perceptron (MLP) classifier outputs a binary decision indicating whether the selected frames are good or bad.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MLP model trained on in-vivo data can effectively classify RF frames as good or bad based on calculated weights.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by specifying training on in-vivo data and mechanism (weights), which clarifies and strengthens the original binary-classifier statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed technique achieves higher quality strain images compared to traditional methods that select pairs of frames that are 1, 2, or 3 frames apart.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed technique achieves higher quality strain images compared to traditional methods that select pairs of frames that are 1, 2, or 3 frames apart.
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim is carried over exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training phase of the algorithm is computationally expensive, taking a few hours, but is performed only once.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training phase of the algorithm is computationally expensive, taking a few hours, but the testing phase is efficient, selecting optimal frame pairs in only 1.9 ms.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the training-cost limitation and adds testing-time efficiency information, clarifying but not contradicting the original limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel convolutional neural network (CNN) classifier model is proposed to classify modulation classes in terms of their families.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel convolutional neural network (CNN) classifier model is proposed to classify modulation classes in terms of their families.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed classifier is robust against realistic wireless channel impairments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed classifier is robust against realistic wireless channel impairments.
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A more comprehensive dataset, named HisarMod2019.1, is introduced, which includes 26 modulation classes and considers real-life applicability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A more comprehensive dataset, named HisarMod2019.1, is introduced, which includes 26 modulation classes and considers real-life applicability.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model performs better than existing models in terms of both accuracy and training time under more realistic conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model performs better than existing models in terms of both accuracy and training time under more realistic conditions.
alignments.rationale: Performance improvements over existing models are preserved as claimed in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model surpassed the performance of existing models when the RadioML2016.10a dataset is utilized.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model surpassed the performance of existing models when the RadioML2016.10a dataset is utilized.
alignments.rationale: This dataset-specific performance claim appears identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Look-Up Table Quantization (LUT-Q) framework allows for flexible network reduction methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Look-Up Table Quantization (LUT-Q) framework allows for the reduction of deep neural networks by learning a value dictionary and an assignment matrix for each layer.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the mechanism (learning dictionaries and assignment matrices) that realizes the flexible reduction capability described in the preprint, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LUT-Q can implement various reduction problems such as non-uniform network quantization, training of multiplierless networks, and network pruning without changing the solver.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LUT-Q is flexible enough to implement various reduction problems such as non-uniform network quantization, training of multiplierless networks, network pruning, or simultaneous quantization and pruning.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same reduction problems and adds additional cases (simultaneous quantization and pruning); it also presents the claim with lower certainty (medium), which tempers the original high-certainty statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method combines gradient descent and a one-step k-means update to iteratively learn value dictionaries and assignment matrices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solver combines gradient descent and a one-step k-means update to iteratively learn the value dictionaries and assignment matrices.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the solver description verbatim, preserving meaning and scope.
